description: We work to prevent the loss of life on the coast and at sea. We produce legislation and guidance on maritime matters, and provide certification to seafarers. MCA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport .

details.body:
We work to prevent the loss of life on the coast and at sea. We produce legislation and guidance on maritime matters, and provide certification to seafarers.

MCA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport.

details.body: The Chief Executive Officer of the Maritime and Coastguard Agency (MCA) is responsible for the overall performance of the MCA, including achieving the goals set out in its business plan. They are also the Accounting Officer for the MCA.

The Director of HM Coastguard is also the Senior Responsible Owner for the UK SAR Helicopter programme.

HM Coastguard coordinate maritime search and rescue (SAR) within the UK SAR region, which covers some 1.25 million square nautical miles of sea and over 10.5 thousand nautical miles of coastline.

HM Coastguard has a variety of resources it can use in response to an emergency situation – whether that is people in distress at sea, or emergencies on the coast or shoreline.

HM Coastguard also has its own volunteer service – the Coastguard Rescue Service, comprising some 3,500 volunteers who form teams that are trained and equipped by HM Coastguard to respond to land-based emergencies such as cliff and mud rescues or searches for missing people.

role_appointments.title: Claire Hughes - Director, Her Majesty's Coastguard

details.body:
The director of UK Customer Maritime Services is responsible for the UK Ship Register, the UK Shipping Concierge, Seafarer Examiners and Seafarer Certification Services, Customer Insight, and Regulatory Innovation.

The directorate’s objectives are to:

  • deliver customer service excellence to all shipowners operating UK-flagged vessels anywhere in the world
  • deliver excellent services to MCA-certified seafarers operating across the globe, including the examination and certification of seafarers
  • represent the wider UK maritime offer through its UK Shipping Concierge service
  • provide customer and market insight to the MCA and other parts of the Civil Service
  • scan the horizon for maritime innovation as an enabler for future-ready maritime regulations

role.title: Director of UK Customer Maritime Services

details.description: Outside these hours or on bank holidays and weekends, for media enquiries ONLY, please send an email outlining your query and putting #Urgent in the subject title.
details.title: Press office

details.description:
Note that this service is for urgent technical enquiries that require action out of office hours only.  This number must not be used for any urgent or routine enquires during normal office hours. 

All enquiries during office hours, including, but not limited to, technical enquiries, enquiries on registration of ships or seafarer’s certification should use the contact details here: https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/maritime-and-coastguard-agency/about/access-and-opening

details.description: Email the MCA's safeguarding officers to report a safeguarding concern. The inbox is monitored Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm.
details.title: Safeguarding officers

details.description:
To raise a concern that is relevant to the work of the MCA.

This can include concerns where there is danger to the environment, health and safety issues, failure to comply with legal obligations, fraud, and breaches of security. 

To make a complaint, please see our complaints procedure: https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/maritime-and-coastguard-agency/about/complaints-procedure.
details.title: Raise a concern – whistleblowing

details.body: The Chief Executive Officer of the Maritime and Coastguard Agency (MCA) is responsible for the overall performance of the MCA, including achieving the goals set out in its business plan. They are also the Accounting Officer for the MCA.

The Director of HM Coastguard is also the Senior Responsible Owner for the UK SAR Helicopter programme.

HM Coastguard coordinate maritime search and rescue (SAR) within the UK SAR region, which covers some 1.25 million square nautical miles of sea and over 10.5 thousand nautical miles of coastline.

HM Coastguard has a variety of resources it can use in response to an emergency situation – whether that is people in distress at sea, or emergencies on the coast or shoreline.

HM Coastguard also has its own volunteer service – the Coastguard Rescue Service, comprising some 3,500 volunteers who form teams that are trained and equipped by HM Coastguard to respond to land-based emergencies such as cliff and mud rescues or searches for missing people.

The Director of UK Maritime Services (formerly Maritime Safety & Standards) is responsible for ensuring the safety and quality of seafarers and ships under the Red Ensign, along with responsibilities for the UK Ship Register and our survey and inspection regime.

The Director Strategy & Corporate Services is responsible for providing the necessary financial, corporate governance, health and safety, risk management, information assurance, corporate development and procurement services to the Agency. This role also delivers the Agency’s information technology, human resources and learning and development.

ordered_roles.title: Director, Strategy and Corporate Services, MCA

details.body: Responsible for reviewing and developing procedures to establish the quality of vessels joining the UK register, maintaining the quality of those vessels already flying the UK Flag, and raising the profile of the register.
